---
title: "Week 9 Project: Sales Data Analyzer"
subtitle: "Building a Professional Data Analysis System"
author: "AI-Integrated Programming Course"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
---

# Project Overview

Create a comprehensive sales analysis system that processes real-world sales data, performs statistical analysis, and generates actionable business insights through clear visualizations and reports.

**Estimated Time:** 6-8 hours  
**Difficulty:** Intermediate to Advanced  
**Key Concepts:** CSV processing, data cleaning, statistical analysis, visualization, report generation

---

# Learning Objectives

By completing this project, you will:

1. Process large CSV files with real-world messiness
2. Clean and validate sales data professionally
3. Calculate meaningful business statistics
4. Create clear, informative visualizations
5. Generate actionable insights from data
6. Build a complete, reusable analysis system

---

# Project Requirements

## Core Features (Required)

Your Sales Data Analyzer must include:

### 1. Data Loading and Parsing
- Read CSV files with various formats (comma, tab, pipe delimited)
- Handle quoted values containing delimiters
- Process files with different encodings
- Skip empty lines and comments
- Validate file structure and headers

### 2. Data Cleaning and Validation
- Clean currency values (remove $, handle different formats)
- Standardize dates to YYYY-MM-DD format
- Validate numeric ranges (prices > 0, quantities > 0)
- Handle missing or invalid data appropriately
- Remove duplicate transactions
- Generate data quality report

### 3. Sales Analysis by Multiple Dimensions

#### By Time Period
- Daily, weekly, monthly sales totals
- Year-over-year growth calculations
- Identify peak and slow periods
- Trend analysis with growth rates

#### By Product
- Total sales per product
- Best and worst selling products
- Product performance rankings
- Inventory turnover insights

#### By Sales Representative
- Individual performance metrics
- Commission calculations
- Performance rankings
- Target achievement analysis

#### By Geographic Region
- Regional sales breakdowns
- Market penetration analysis
- Regional growth patterns
- Opportunity identification

### 4. Statistical Calculations
- Mean, median, mode of transaction values
- Standard deviation and variance
- Percentile calculations (25th, 75th, 90th)
- Outlier detection and analysis
- Correlation between variables

### 5. Visualizations (Text-Based)
- Horizontal bar charts for comparisons
- Trend lines for time series
- Distribution histograms
- Comparison tables
- Performance dashboards

### 6. Report Generation
- Executive summary with key metrics
- Detailed breakdowns by dimension
- Actionable recommendations
- Export capabilities (CSV, text report)

## Enhanced Features (Optional)

If you complete the core features, add:

### 1. Advanced Analytics
- Seasonal pattern detection
- Moving averages (7-day, 30-day)
- Sales forecasting (simple trend projection)
- Customer lifetime value estimation
- Market basket analysis (products bought together)

### 2. Interactive Features
- Menu-driven analysis options
- Custom date range selection
- Dynamic filtering capabilities
- Real-time data updates
- Analysis history tracking

### 3. Performance Optimization
- Handle files with 100,000+ rows
- Efficient memory usage
- Progress indicators for long operations
- Parallel processing for multiple files

### 4. Business Intelligence
- Automated anomaly detection
- Smart insights generation
- Competitive benchmarking
- Goal tracking and alerts

---

# Data Format Specifications

## Input CSV Format

Your analyzer should handle this standard format:

```csv
TransactionID,Date,ProductID,ProductName,Category,Quantity,UnitPrice,TotalAmount,SalesRep,Region,CustomerID
1001,2024-01-15,P-101,Laptop Pro,Electronics,1,999.99,999.99,John Smith,North,C-5001
1002,2024-01-15,P-205,"Wireless Mouse, Premium",Accessories,2,29.99,59.98,Jane Doe,North,C-5002
1003,2024-01-16,P-101,Laptop Pro,Electronics,1,999.99,999.99,Bob Johnson,South,C-5003
```

## Common Data Issues to Handle

```csv
# Missing values
1004,2024-01-17,P-301,,Office,1,49.99,49.99,Alice Brown,East,C-5004

# Currency formatting
1005,2024-01-17,P-401,Monitor,"Electronics",1,"$299.00","$299.00","John Smith","North",C-5005

# Date variations
1006,01/18/2024,P-501,Keyboard,Accessories,1,$79.99,$79.99,Jane Doe,West,C-5006
1007,18-01-2024,P-601,Speaker Set,Electronics,1,149.99,149.99,Bob Johnson,South,C-5007

# Quoted values with commas
1008,2024-01-19,P-701,"Desk Lamp, LED",Office,3,39.99,119.97,"Alice Brown",East,C-5008
```

---

# Implementation Guide

## Step 1: Project Structure

Organize your code into logical sections:

```python
"""
Sales Data Analyzer
Professional sales analysis system for business insights
"""

# =========================================
# CONFIGURATION
# =========================================

# Column mappings for flexibility
COLUMN_MAPPINGS = {
    "date": ["Date", "TransactionDate", "SaleDate"],
    "product": ["ProductName", "Product", "Item"],
    "amount": ["TotalAmount", "Total", "Revenue"],
    "quantity": ["Quantity", "Qty", "Units"],
    "rep": ["SalesRep", "Representative", "Salesperson"],
    "region": ["Region", "Territory", "Area"]
}

# =========================================
# DATA LOADING FUNCTIONS
# =========================================

def detect_delimiter(filename, sample_lines=5):
    """Automatically detect CSV delimiter."""
    # Implementation here
    pass

def read_sales_csv(filename):
    """Read sales data from CSV file."""
    # Implementation here
    pass

# =========================================
# DATA CLEANING FUNCTIONS
# =========================================

def clean_sales_data(raw_data):
    """Clean and validate sales data."""
    # Implementation here
    pass

# =========================================
# ANALYSIS FUNCTIONS
# =========================================

def analyze_by_time_period(data, period="daily"):
    """Analyze sales by time period."""
    # Implementation here
    pass

def analyze_by_product(data):
    """Analyze sales by product."""
    # Implementation here
    pass

# =========================================
# VISUALIZATION FUNCTIONS
# =========================================

def create_sales_dashboard(analyses):
    """Create comprehensive sales dashboard."""
    # Implementation here
    pass

# =========================================
# MAIN PROGRAM
# =========================================

def main():
    """Main program coordinator."""
    # Implementation here
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

## Step 2: Core Data Processing

### CSV Reading with Flexibility

```python
def read_sales_csv(filename, encoding="utf-8"):
    """
    Read sales data from CSV with automatic format detection.
    
    Returns:
        List of dictionaries with standardized column names
    """
    print(f"📂 Loading data from {filename}...")
    
    try:
        # Detect delimiter
        with open(filename, "r", encoding=encoding) as f:
            sample = f.readline()
        
        delimiter = detect_delimiter([sample])
        
        # Read full file
        with open(filename, "r", encoding=encoding) as f:
            lines = f.readlines()
        
        if not lines:
            print("❌ Error: File is empty")
            return []
        
        # Parse headers
        headers = parse_csv_line(lines[0].strip(), delimiter)
        standardized_headers = standardize_column_names(headers)
        
        # Parse data
        data = []
        for line_num, line in enumerate(lines[1:], start=2):
            if not line.strip() or line.startswith("#"):
                continue
            
            values = parse_csv_line(line.strip(), delimiter)
            
            if len(values) != len(headers):
                print(f"⚠️  Warning: Line {line_num} has {len(values)} values, expected {len(headers)}")
                continue
            
            row = {}
            for i, header in enumerate(standardized_headers):
                row[header] = values[i] if i < len(values) else ""
            
            data.append(row)
        
        print(f"✅ Loaded {len(data)} records successfully")
        return data
    
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Error reading file: {e}")
        return []
```

### Data Cleaning Pipeline

```python
def clean_sales_data(raw_data):
    """
    Clean and validate sales data.
    
    Returns:
        Tuple of (cleaned_data, quality_report)
    """
    cleaned_data = []
    quality_report = {
        "total_records": len(raw_data),
        "cleaned_records": 0,
        "invalid_records": 0,
        "missing_values": 0,
        "duplicates_removed": 0,
        "issues": []
    }
    
    seen_transactions = set()
    
    for row_num, row in enumerate(raw_data, start=1):
        # Create cleaned row
        cleaned_row = {}
        row_valid = True
        
        # Clean each field
        # Transaction ID
        transaction_id = row.get("transaction_id", "").strip()
        if not transaction_id:
            quality_report["issues"].append(f"Row {row_num}: Missing transaction ID")
            row_valid = False
        else:
            # Check for duplicates
            if transaction_id in seen_transactions:
                quality_report["duplicates_removed"] += 1
                continue
            seen_transactions.add(transaction_id)
            cleaned_row["transaction_id"] = transaction_id
        
        # Date cleaning
        date_str = row.get("date", "")
        cleaned_date = standardize_date(date_str)
        if not cleaned_date:
            quality_report["issues"].append(f"Row {row_num}: Invalid date '{date_str}'")
            row_valid = False
        else:
            cleaned_row["date"] = cleaned_date
        
        # Amount cleaning
        amount_str = row.get("amount", "")
        cleaned_amount = clean_currency(amount_str)
        if cleaned_amount <= 0:
            quality_report["issues"].append(f"Row {row_num}: Invalid amount '{amount_str}'")
            row_valid = False
        else:
            cleaned_row["amount"] = cleaned_amount
        
        # Continue with other fields...
        
        if row_valid:
            cleaned_data.append(cleaned_row)
            quality_report["cleaned_records"] += 1
        else:
            quality_report["invalid_records"] += 1
    
    return cleaned_data, quality_report
```

## Step 3: Analysis Implementation

### Time-Based Analysis

```python
def analyze_by_time_period(data, period="daily"):
    """
    Analyze sales by time period.
    
    Args:
        data: Cleaned sales data
        period: "daily", "weekly", "monthly", "yearly"
    
    Returns:
        Dictionary with time-based analysis
    """
    from collections import defaultdict
    
    period_sales = defaultdict(float)
    period_transactions = defaultdict(int)
    
    for row in data:
        date = row.get("date", "")
        amount = row.get("amount", 0)
        
        # Determine period key
        if period == "daily":
            period_key = date
        elif period == "weekly":
            # Calculate week number
            year, month, day = date.split("-")
            week_num = (int(day) - 1) // 7 + 1
            period_key = f"{year}-{month}-W{week_num}"
        elif period == "monthly":
            period_key = date[:7]  # YYYY-MM
        elif period == "yearly":
            period_key = date[:4]  # YYYY
        
        period_sales[period_key] += amount
        period_transactions[period_key] += 1
    
    # Calculate statistics
    periods = sorted(period_sales.keys())
    values = [period_sales[p] for p in periods]
    
    analysis = {
        "period_type": period,
        "periods": periods,
        "sales": {p: period_sales[p] for p in periods},
        "transactions": {p: period_transactions[p] for p in periods},
        "statistics": calculate_statistics(values),
        "growth_rates": calculate_growth_rates(periods, period_sales),
        "peak_period": max(period_sales, key=period_sales.get) if period_sales else None,
        "slow_period": min(period_sales, key=period_sales.get) if period_sales else None
    }
    
    return analysis

def calculate_growth_rates(periods, period_sales):
    """Calculate period-over-period growth rates."""
    growth_rates = {}
    
    for i in range(1, len(periods)):
        prev_period = periods[i-1]
        curr_period = periods[i]
        
        prev_sales = period_sales[prev_period]
        curr_sales = period_sales[curr_period]
        
        if prev_sales > 0:
            growth_rate = ((curr_sales - prev_sales) / prev_sales) * 100
            growth_rates[curr_period] = round(growth_rate, 2)
    
    return growth_rates
```

## Step 4: Visualization System

### Professional Dashboard Creation

```python
def create_sales_dashboard(data, analyses):
    """
    Create comprehensive sales dashboard.
    """
    dashboard = []
    
    # Header
    dashboard.append("=" * 80)
    dashboard.append("                           SALES ANALYSIS DASHBOARD")
    dashboard.append("=" * 80)
    dashboard.append("")
    
    # Executive Summary
    total_sales = sum(row.get("amount", 0) for row in data)
    total_transactions = len(data)
    avg_transaction = total_sales / total_transactions if total_transactions > 0 else 0
    
    dashboard.append("📊 EXECUTIVE SUMMARY")
    dashboard.append("-" * 40)
    dashboard.append(f"Total Revenue:        ${total_sales:,.2f}")
    dashboard.append(f"Total Transactions:   {total_transactions:,}")
    dashboard.append(f"Average Transaction:  ${avg_transaction:,.2f}")
    dashboard.append("")
    
    # Time Analysis
    if "time_analysis" in analyses:
        time_data = analyses["time_analysis"]
        dashboard.append("📅 SALES TREND (Last 30 Days)")
        dashboard.append("-" * 40)
        
        # Create mini line chart
        sales_by_day = time_data["sales"]
        if sales_by_day:
            chart = create_trend_chart(sales_by_day, height=10, width=60)
            dashboard.extend(chart.split("\n"))
        dashboard.append("")
    
    # Product Analysis
    if "product_analysis" in analyses:
        product_data = analyses["product_analysis"]
        dashboard.append("🏆 TOP PRODUCTS")
        dashboard.append("-" * 40)
        
        # Top 5 products bar chart
        top_products = dict(sorted(product_data["product_sales"].items(), 
                                 key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5])
        
        chart = create_bar_chart(top_products, show_values=True, max_width=40)
        dashboard.extend(chart.split("\n"))
        dashboard.append("")
    
    # Regional Analysis
    if "regional_analysis" in analyses:
        regional_data = analyses["regional_analysis"]
        dashboard.append("🌍 REGIONAL PERFORMANCE")
        dashboard.append("-" * 40)
        
        # Regional comparison
        regional_sales = regional_data["region_sales"]
        chart = create_bar_chart(regional_sales, show_values=True, max_width=40)
        dashboard.extend(chart.split("\n"))
        dashboard.append("")
    
    # Sales Rep Performance
    if "rep_analysis" in analyses:
        rep_data = analyses["rep_analysis"]
        dashboard.append("👥 TOP SALES REPRESENTATIVES")
        dashboard.append("-" * 40)
        
        # Top performers table
        top_reps = sorted(rep_data["rep_performance"].items(), 
                         key=lambda x: x[1]["total_sales"], reverse=True)[:5]
        
        dashboard.append(f"{'Name':<20} {'Sales':<15} {'Transactions':<12} {'Avg Sale':<10}")
        dashboard.append("-" * 60)
        
        for rep, stats in top_reps:
            dashboard.append(f"{rep:<20} ${stats['total_sales']:<14,.2f} "
                           f"{stats['count']:<12} ${stats['avg_sale']:<10,.2f}")
        dashboard.append("")
    
    # Key Insights
    dashboard.append("💡 KEY INSIGHTS & RECOMMENDATIONS")
    dashboard.append("-" * 40)
    
    insights = generate_insights(data, analyses)
    for insight in insights:
        dashboard.append(f"• {insight}")
    
    dashboard.append("")
    dashboard.append("=" * 80)
    dashboard.append(f"Report generated on: {get_current_timestamp()}")
    
    return "\n".join(dashboard)

def generate_insights(data, analyses):
    """Generate actionable insights from analysis."""
    insights = []
    
    # Time-based insights
    if "time_analysis" in analyses:
        time_data = analyses["time_analysis"]
        if time_data.get("peak_period"):
            insights.append(f"Peak sales period: {time_data['peak_period']} - "
                          f"Consider increasing inventory")
        
        # Check for growth trends
        growth_rates = time_data.get("growth_rates", {})
        if growth_rates:
            recent_growth = list(growth_rates.values())[-1]
            if recent_growth > 10:
                insights.append(f"Strong growth of {recent_growth}% - "
                              f"Momentum is positive")
            elif recent_growth < -10:
                insights.append(f"Sales declined {abs(recent_growth)}% - "
                              f"Investigate causes")
    
    # Product insights
    if "product_analysis" in analyses:
        product_data = analyses["product_analysis"]
        best_seller = product_data.get("best_seller")
        if best_seller:
            insights.append(f"'{best_seller}' is the top performer - "
                          f"Ensure adequate stock")
        
        # Low performers
        poor_sellers = product_data.get("poor_sellers", [])
        if poor_sellers:
            insights.append(f"Consider promotions for slow-moving items: "
                          f"{', '.join(poor_sellers[:3])}")
    
    # Regional insights
    if "regional_analysis" in analyses:
        regional_data = analyses["regional_analysis"]
        underperforming = regional_data.get("underperforming_regions", [])
        if underperforming:
            insights.append(f"Focus on growth in: {', '.join(underperforming)}")
    
    return insights
```

## Step 5: Complete Implementation Example

```python
def main():
    """
    Main program - Sales Data Analyzer
    """
    print("🚀 SALES DATA ANALYZER v1.0")
    print("=" * 50)
    
    # Get filename
    filename = input("Enter CSV filename (or press Enter for 'sales.csv'): ").strip()
    if not filename:
        filename = "sales.csv"
    
    # Load data
    raw_data = read_sales_csv(filename)
    if not raw_data:
        print("❌ Failed to load data. Exiting.")
        return
    
    # Clean data
    print("\n🧹 Cleaning data...")
    cleaned_data, quality_report = clean_sales_data(raw_data)
    
    # Show data quality report
    print("\n📋 DATA QUALITY REPORT")
    print("-" * 30)
    print(f"Total records:     {quality_report['total_records']}")
    print(f"Clean records:     {quality_report['cleaned_records']}")
    print(f"Invalid records:   {quality_report['invalid_records']}")
    print(f"Duplicates removed: {quality_report['duplicates_removed']}")
    
    if not cleaned_data:
        print("❌ No valid data to analyze. Exiting.")
        return
    
    # Perform analyses
    print("\n🔍 Performing analysis...")
    
    analyses = {
        "time_analysis": analyze_by_time_period(cleaned_data, "daily"),
        "product_analysis": analyze_by_product(cleaned_data),
        "regional_analysis": analyze_by_region(cleaned_data),
        "rep_analysis": analyze_by_sales_rep(cleaned_data)
    }
    
    # Generate dashboard
    print("\n📊 Generating dashboard...")
    dashboard = create_sales_dashboard(cleaned_data, analyses)
    
    # Display dashboard
    print("\n" + dashboard)
    
    # Export options
    while True:
        print("\n📁 EXPORT OPTIONS")
        print("1. Save dashboard to text file")
        print("2. Export cleaned data to CSV")
        print("3. Export detailed analysis")
        print("4. Exit")
        
        choice = input("\nYour choice (1-4): ").strip()
        
        if choice == "1":
            export_dashboard(dashboard)
        elif choice == "2":
            export_cleaned_data(cleaned_data)
        elif choice == "3":
            export_detailed_analysis(analyses)
        elif choice == "4":
            print("\n✅ Thank you for using Sales Data Analyzer!")
            break
        else:
            print("Invalid choice. Please try again.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

---

# Testing Requirements

Create comprehensive tests for your analyzer:

```python
def test_sales_analyzer():
    """Test suite for sales analyzer."""
    print("🧪 Running Sales Analyzer Tests")
    print("=" * 40)
    
    # Test 1: CSV parsing
    print("\n--- Test 1: CSV Parsing ---")
    test_csv = '"Product, A",100.50,"Smith, John"'
    parsed = parse_csv_line(test_csv)
    assert parsed == ["Product, A", "100.50", "Smith, John"]
    print("✓ CSV parsing with quotes")
    
    # Test 2: Date cleaning
    print("\n--- Test 2: Date Standardization ---")
    test_dates = [
        ("01/15/2024", "2024-01-15"),
        ("15-01-2024", "2024-01-15"),
        ("2024.01.15", "2024-01-15"),
        ("Jan 15, 2024", "2024-01-15")
    ]
    
    for input_date, expected in test_dates:
        result = standardize_date(input_date)
        assert result == expected, f"Failed: {input_date} → {result}"
    print("✓ Date standardization")
    
    # Test 3: Currency cleaning
    print("\n--- Test 3: Currency Cleaning ---")
    test_amounts = [
        ("$1,234.56", 1234.56),
        ("€1.234,56", 1234.56),
        ("($100.00)", -100.00),
        ("1.5K", 1500.0)
    ]
    
    for input_amount, expected in test_amounts:
        result = clean_currency(input_amount)
        assert abs(result - expected) < 0.01, f"Failed: {input_amount} → {result}"
    print("✓ Currency cleaning")
    
    # Test 4: Statistical calculations
    print("\n--- Test 4: Statistics ---")
    test_values = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
    stats = calculate_statistics(test_values)
    assert stats["mean"] == 30
    assert stats["median"] == 30
    assert stats["min"] == 10
    assert stats["max"] == 50
    print("✓ Statistical calculations")
    
    # Test 5: Growth rate calculation
    print("\n--- Test 5: Growth Rates ---")
    periods = ["2024-01", "2024-02", "2024-03"]
    sales = {"2024-01": 1000, "2024-02": 1100, "2024-03": 1210}
    growth = calculate_growth_rates(periods, sales)
    assert growth["2024-02"] == 10.0  # 10% growth
    assert growth["2024-03"] == 10.0  # 10% growth
    print("✓ Growth rate calculations")
    
    print("\n🎉 All tests passed!")

# Run tests
test_sales_analyzer()
```

---

# Submission Requirements

## What to Submit

1. **Main Program** (`sales_analyzer.py`)
   - Complete implementation of all core features
   - Clean, well-organized code
   - Comprehensive error handling
   - Professional output formatting

2. **Test Suite** (`test_analyzer.py`)
   - Tests for all major functions
   - Edge case coverage
   - Sample data generation
   - Validation of calculations

3. **Sample Output** (`analysis_report.txt`)
   - Example dashboard output
   - Shows all visualizations
   - Demonstrates insights generation
   - Professional formatting

4. **Documentation** (`README.txt`)
   - How to run the analyzer
   - Input file format requirements
   - Feature descriptions
   - Known limitations

5. **Sample Data** (`sample_sales.csv`)
   - Minimum 100 transactions
   - Multiple products, reps, regions
   - Some messy data to demonstrate cleaning
   - Realistic business scenarios

---

# Grading Rubric

## Core Functionality (50%)

| Feature | Points | Criteria |
|---------|---------|----------|
| CSV Processing | 10 | Handles quotes, delimiters, encodings |
| Data Cleaning | 10 | Comprehensive validation and cleaning |
| Statistical Analysis | 10 | Accurate calculations, multiple measures |
| Visualizations | 10 | Clear, scaled, informative charts |
| Report Generation | 10 | Professional output with insights |

## Code Quality (30%)

| Aspect | Points | Criteria |
|--------|---------|----------|
| Organization | 10 | Logical structure, clear functions |
| Error Handling | 10 | Graceful failures, helpful messages |
| Documentation | 10 | Clear comments, function docstrings |

## Analysis Quality (20%)

| Element | Points | Criteria |
|---------|---------|----------|
| Insights | 10 | Meaningful, actionable recommendations |
| Completeness | 5 | All dimensions analyzed |
| Presentation | 5 | Professional, clear formatting |

---

# Tips for Success

## Data Processing
- Test with messy data early
- Handle edge cases gracefully
- Validate everything
- Provide clear error messages

## Analysis Design
- Think like a business analyst
- Focus on actionable insights
- Make comparisons meaningful
- Highlight important findings

## Visualization
- Keep charts simple and clear
- Use appropriate scales
- Label everything
- Show context (totals, percentages)

## Code Organization
- Separate concerns clearly
- Make functions reusable
- Test each component
- Document your approach

---

# Extension Ideas

Once core features work:

1. **Predictive Analytics**
   - Simple sales forecasting
   - Seasonal trend detection
   - Anomaly identification

2. **Customer Analysis**
   - Customer segmentation
   - Purchase patterns
   - Loyalty metrics

3. **Interactive Features**
   - Custom query builder
   - Dynamic filtering
   - Real-time updates

4. **Performance Features**
   - Multi-file processing
   - Incremental analysis
   - Memory optimization

Remember: Focus on building a tool that provides real business value!

---

# Getting Help

If you need assistance:

1. Review Week 9 lecture materials
2. Check lab exercises for similar patterns
3. Use AI with appropriate constraints
4. Break problems into smaller functions
5. Test with simple data first

Good luck creating your professional sales analyzer! 📊💼
